notes: runs perfectly apart from the mouse issue. (i have not tested controllers)
title: My Summer Car

notes: Runs out of the box, no problems discovered after several hours of playing.
title: My Summer Car

notes: Mouse drift issue still happens when scrolling, which one will do often in this game. If you have a mouse with customizable DPI, you can turn the in-game mouse sensitivity all the way down and turn your DPI up very high to get a pretty normal experience. This eliminates the drift down to an imperceptible level. Gamepads do seem to work fine, though all I had to test with was an XBOne controller which the game doesn't seem to provide quite enough customization for to my liking. Though it does function.
title: My Summer Car

notes: As others have noted, the mouse likes to drift down and right. Otherwise seems playable. I only tried windowed mode.
title: My Summer Car

notes: Still has problem with mouse drifting down-right every mouse click, or mousewheel scroll. Still has no support for controllers. Otherwise is roughly playable with Keyboard and Mouse.
title: My Summer Car

notes: As of Proton 3.16-8, the mouse drifting issue has been eliminated. The game now runs flawlessly as far as I can tell.
title: My Summer Car

notes: Under Proton 3.16-8, the mouse pointer drifting down-right has gone, and the game is now even recognising an XBox 360 Controller, and roughly playable with it - though it won't currently recognise my Logitech G920 Wheel at all (Running the game in Linux with a swapped Unity Executable will recognise the wheel, and other Proton games have recognised it fine, so I'm not sure what the issue is yet).
title: My Summer Car

notes: Game runs great and with latest proton the mouse drift issue is gone, if playing with just a keyboard its platinum worthy, but the bugginess surrounding controller input and how driving in the game greatly improves with a controller causes issues.
title: My Summer Car

notes: Everything works as supposed and performance is great. I haven't tested controllers but with keyboard+mouse combo it works without issues.

title: My Summer Car

notes: Mouse works as expected with no drift or sticking issues, framerate is decent. With Proton 4.11-1, it works fine with a Logitech Momo Racing wheel, including force feedback (I didn't touch the controls in the Unity launcher, just within the game). Pedals need to be held before assigning their axes as suggested by the text on the game's configuration screen. I disabled all dead zones, set wheel rotation to 240 to visually match the Momo wheel, turned on force feedback with multiplier and clamp both set to 100, and force invert on. Buttons and shifter can be assigned as desired.
title: My Summer Car

notes: It just works, d9vk recommended for slightly higher FPS but not really noticeable.
title: My Summer Car

notes: Literally everything works how you'd expect.
title: My Summer Car

notes: I tried d9vk as another report suggested (with the game's "Golden Eye" graphics setting) and it caused shadows to glitch out, especially inside the house, although it did slightly improve framerate and reduce CPU load. With the default (i.e. not d9vk), there are no graphical glitches and performance is still acceptable.
